Operation 'get_v4_historical_sports_sport_odds' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity about the historical nature of the data and availability windows. Added temporal context about snapshot intervals and availability dates to help AI agents understand data freshness and historical scope. Clarified that this returns a snapshot at a specific time, not current odds.

data.current_description: Returns list of live and upcoming events at a point in time for a given sport, including bookmaker odds for the specified region and markets. This endpoint will only return featured markets (moneyline, match winner, 1x2, head-to-head, spreads, totals, over/under, outrights etc.), using the market keys `h2h`, `spreads`, `totals`. This endpoint was previously `/v4/sports/{sport}/odds-history`, which has been deprecated.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve historical odds for featured markets at a specific time
data.description_suggestion: Returns a historical snapshot of live and upcoming events for a given sport at a specified point in time, including bookmaker odds for the specified region and markets. This endpoint returns only featured markets (moneyline, match winner, 1x2, head-to-head, spreads, totals, over/under, outrights) using market keys `h2h`, `spreads`, `totals`. Historical snapshots are available from June 2020 at 10-minute intervals until September 2022, and at 5-minute intervals thereafter. Note: This endpoint replaces the deprecated `/v4/sports/{sport}/odds-history`.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks clarity about the historical nature of the data and availability windows. Added temporal context about snapshot intervals and availability dates to help AI agents understand data freshness and historical scope. Clarified that this returns a snapshot at a specific time, not current odds.

message: Operation 'get_v4_historical_sports_sport_events' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Missing summary entirely. Description is minimal and lacks context about use cases and relationship to other endpoints. Added guidance on when to use this endpoint versus related historical endpoints to improve AI agent decision-making about endpoint selection.

data.current_description: Returns a list of events for the specified sport as they appeared at the specified timestamp (date parameter). Odds are not included in the response
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve historical events for a sport at a specific timestamp
data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of events for the specified sport as they appeared at the specified timestamp (date parameter). This endpoint provides historical event data without odds information. Use this to retrieve event details at a specific point in time, or use `/v4/historical/sports/{sport}/odds` to get odds for featured markets or `/v4/historical/sports/{sport}/events/{eventId}/odds` for specific event odds.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is minimal and lacks context about use cases and relationship to other endpoints. Added guidance on when to use this endpoint versus related historical endpoints to improve AI agent decision-making about endpoint selection.

message: Operation 'get_v4_historical_sports_sport_events_eventId_odds' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Missing summary entirely. Description lacks temporal context about data availability for different market types. Added clarity about snapshot intervals for non-featured markets (May 2023 onwards) and distinguished this endpoint's flexibility from the featured-markets-only endpoint to help AI agents select the appropriate endpoint based on market type requirements.

data.current_description: Returns bookmaker odds for a single event as they appeared at the specified timestamp (date parameter). Accepts any available market keys using the `markets` parameter. Use this endpoint to access historical odds from any available markets for an event. For historical odds of the most popular featured markets, use the `/v4/historical/sports/{sport}/odds` endpoint. See the [full list of market keys here](https://the-odds-api.com/sports-odds-data/betting-markets.html)
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve historical odds for a specific event at a given time
data.description_suggestion: Returns bookmaker odds for a single event as they appeared at the specified timestamp (date parameter). Accepts any available market keys using the `markets` parameter, allowing access to featured markets, player props, period markets, and alternates. Historical snapshots for non-featured markets are available from May 2023 at 5-minute intervals. Use this endpoint for comprehensive historical odds data on a specific event; use `/v4/historical/sports/{sport}/odds` for featured markets across multiple events. Refer to the [full list of market keys](https://the-odds-api.com/sports-odds-data/betting-markets.html) for available options.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description lacks temporal context about data availability for different market types. Added clarity about snapshot intervals for non-featured markets (May 2023 onwards) and distinguished this endpoint's flexibility from the featured-markets-only endpoint to help AI agents select the appropriate endpoint based on market type requirements.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, unclear_purpose, missing_input_details. Missing summary entirely. Description is incomplete - it doesn't explain the operation's purpose (discovering available sports), what the sport keys are used for, or mention the available parameters. AI agents need to understand this is a discovery endpoint and how to use the results.

data.current_description: A successful response includes a list of available sports and tournaments
data.summary_suggestion: List all available sports and tournaments
data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of all in-season sports and tournaments available through the API. Use this endpoint to discover which sports are currently supported and to obtain the sport keys needed for subsequent odds and events requests. Accepts optional parameters to filter results.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is incomplete - it doesn't explain the operation's purpose (discovering available sports), what the sport keys are used for, or mention the available parameters. AI agents need to understand this is a discovery endpoint and how to use the results.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_odds' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about what 'featured markets' means, doesn't mention that sport key is required, and doesn't explain the relationship to the single-event odds endpoint. AI agents need to understand this is for featured markets vs. the more comprehensive single-event endpoint.

data.current_description: Returns list of live and upcoming events for a given sport, showing bookmaker odds for the specified region and markets
data.summary_suggestion: Get current odds for live and upcoming events
data.description_suggestion: Returns live and upcoming events for a specified sport with current bookmaker odds across featured markets and regions. Includes event details (teams, commence time) and odds from multiple bookmakers. Use the sport key from /v4/sports endpoint. Supports filtering by region and market selection. This is the primary endpoint for accessing featured betting markets.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description lacks clarity about what 'featured markets' means, doesn't mention that sport key is required, and doesn't explain the relationship to the single-event odds endpoint. AI agents need to understand this is for featured markets vs. the more comprehensive single-event endpoint.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_scores' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description doesn't explain how to retrieve historical scores (days_from parameter), doesn't clarify that odds are excluded, and doesn't mention the sport key requirement. AI agents need to understand this is scores-only and how to access historical data.

data.current_description: Returns list of live and upcoming events for a given sport, and optionally recently completed events. Live and completed events will contain scores
data.summary_suggestion: Get event scores for live and completed matches
data.description_suggestion: Returns live, upcoming, and optionally recently completed events for a specified sport with current scores. Live and finished events include score information. Use the sport key from /v4/sports endpoint. Supports filtering by days back to retrieve historical scores. Does not include bookmaker odds - use /v4/sports/{sport}/odds for odds data.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description doesn't explain how to retrieve historical scores (days_from parameter), doesn't clarify that odds are excluded, and doesn't mention the sport key requirement. AI agents need to understand this is scores-only and how to access historical data.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_events' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description mentions quota exemption but doesn't explain the practical use case. Doesn't clarify what information is returned or how to use event IDs with other endpoints. AI agents need to understand when to use this lightweight endpoint vs. the full odds endpoint.

data.current_description: Returns a list of in-play and pre-match events for a specified sport or league. Odds are not included in the response. This endpoint does not count against the usage quota.
data.summary_suggestion: List in-play and pre-match events without quota usage
data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of in-play and pre-match events for a specified sport or league without bookmaker odds. This is a lightweight endpoint useful for discovering available events and does not consume API quota. Returns event identifiers, teams/participants, and commence times. Use event IDs with /v4/sports/{sport}/events/{eventId}/odds to retrieve odds for specific events.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description mentions quota exemption but doesn't explain the practical use case. Doesn't clarify what information is returned or how to use event IDs with other endpoints. AI agents need to understand when to use this lightweight endpoint vs. the full odds endpoint.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_events_eventId_odds' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details. Missing summary. Description is verbose and doesn't clearly explain the key difference from the bulk odds endpoint or how to specify markets. AI agents need clearer guidance on when to use this endpoint and how to construct market requests.

data.current_description: Returns bookmaker odds for a single event as long as the event is still valid (live or upcoming). Accepts any available market keys using the `markets` parameter. Use this endpoint to access any available markets for an event. For the most popular featured markets, use the `/v4/sports/{sport}/odds` endpoint (see above). The API is gradually expanding coverage to more betting markets. [See the full list here](https://the-odds-api.com/sports-odds-data/betting-markets.html)
data.summary_suggestion: Get comprehensive odds for a specific event
data.description_suggestion: Returns bookmaker odds for a single event across any available markets. Accepts market keys via the markets parameter to filter specific betting markets. Only returns odds for live or upcoming events. Use this endpoint for comprehensive market access beyond featured markets available in /v4/sports/{sport}/odds. Requires valid sport key and event ID. Supports region and bookmaker filtering.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is verbose and doesn't clearly explain the key difference from the bulk odds endpoint or how to specify markets. AI agents need clearer guidance on when to use this endpoint and how to construct market requests.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_events_eventId_markets' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description doesn't explain the practical use case (discovering markets before requesting odds) or clarify that this is a discovery endpoint. AI agents need to understand this is a prerequisite step for the odds endpoint and that market availability is dynamic.

data.current_description: Returns available market keys for each bookmaker for a single event. This endpoint only returns recently seen market keys for each bookmaker - it is not a comprehensive list of all supported markets. As an event's commence time approaches, this endpoint will return more market keys as bookmaker's open more markets.
data.summary_suggestion: Discover available betting markets for an event
data.description_suggestion: Returns the available market keys for each bookmaker for a single event. This endpoint provides recently observed markets and is not exhaustive - additional markets may become available as the event commence time approaches. Use this to discover which markets are currently offered before requesting odds via /v4/sports/{sport}/events/{eventId}/odds. Requires valid sport key and event ID.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description doesn't explain the practical use case (discovering markets before requesting odds) or clarify that this is a discovery endpoint. AI agents need to understand this is a prerequisite step for the odds endpoint and that market availability is dynamic.

message: Operation 'get_v4_sports_sport_participants' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Missing summary. Description is verbose with examples but lacks clarity about the practical use case and doesn't mention the sport key requirement. AI agents need to understand this is a reference endpoint and how it relates to event definitions.

data.current_description:
Returns a list of participants for a given sport. Depending on the sport, a participant can be either a team or an individual. For example for NBA, this endpoint returns a list of teams. For tennis, it will return a list of players.
Participants are used to define events, for example Boston Celtics vs Chicago Bulls, or Novak Djokovic vs Roger Federer. This endpoint does not return players on a team.
The returning list should be considered a whitelist and may include participants that are no longer active. Future updates will remove inactive particpants.
data.summary_suggestion: List teams or players for a sport
data.description_suggestion: Returns a list of all participants (teams or individual athletes) for a specified sport. Participants form the basis of events - for team sports like NBA, returns teams; for individual sports like tennis, returns players. The list serves as a whitelist of valid participants and may include inactive participants. Does not return team rosters. Use the sport key from /v4/sports endpoint.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description is verbose with examples but lacks clarity about the practical use case and doesn't mention the sport key requirement. AI agents need to understand this is a reference endpoint and how it relates to event definitions.
